projectTitle_es: Consultoría Digital y Resolución de Problemas

downloadCta.headline: Ready to move forward?
downloadCta.subtitle: This is what working with ZNAS looks like in practice. The full proposal is below.

closingNote: Investment terms are reviewed during the alignment meeting and confirmed in writing before any work begins.

summary.headline: Haven has 20 years of work behind it. This proposal is about what gets built around it next.
summary.body: ZNAS comes in as a consultant and a fixer for Haven's digital and technological side. The starting point is the suspended Google Business Profiles. From there, the work continues across whatever digital and technological problems are holding the business back, with Ángel approving each step.

howItWorks.intro: José leads the engagement directly. The work covers technology decisions, execution, and recovery of the suspended Google Business Profiles from week one. Specific scope and pricing are confirmed in the alignment meeting before any work begins.

cards.title: Month-to-month engagement
cards.body: No long-term commitments. Pause anytime with 15 days' notice.

cards.title: Ángel approves every step
cards.body: ZNAS proposes and recommends; Haven gives the green light before anything is executed.

cards.title: Three phases, one logic
cards.body: Assessment, Transformation Proposal, Execution. Each phase ends with a clear deliverable: a diagnosis, an approved roadmap, and a working infrastructure. Scope is defined as the work progresses, not promised upfront.

cards.title: 100% IP ownership
cards.body: Everything ZNAS builds for Haven is owned by Haven. No retained rights.

team.name: José Sanz
team.tagline: 17+ years building production-scale systems. Founder of ZNAS LLC, co-founder of Visionarys. MIT Certification in AI Business Strategy.
team.caption: José leads the engagement directly, supported by the ZNAS team where execution requires it.

initiative.headline: Initiative on paper.
initiative.body:
Before any agreement is signed, the operational gameplan for Haven's most urgent problem is already drafted. The document below walks through how ZNAS would attack the four suspended Google Business Profiles: what we can start on this week without any client access, what we'll need from your team to unblock the rest, and the one task only Ángel can handle (a single 60-second on-camera verification that mirrors a real reinstated case from a peer business).

It is research-backed: cross-referenced against documented reinstatement cases, validated against Google's current SAB standard, and structured so nothing burns one of the limited appeal attempts. More than that, it is the kind of preparation we bring to every problem ZNAS takes on. You will see how each task is owned, what is blocked on what, and the exact sequence we would run from kickoff to first response from Google.

Read it before our alignment meeting so we can spend that time on adjustments instead of explanations.
